EFL TO IMPOSE 'ROONEY RULE' NEXT SEASON

Football League clubs will have to interview at least one Black, Asian or ethnic minority candidate for a first-team manager's position from next season.

It follows an 18-month trial period, which saw clubs make a voluntary commitment to the rule.

The 'Rooney Rule' is an NFL policy that requires the league's 32 teams to interview BAME candidates for head coaching and senior operation roles.

"The EFL has today made a further commitment to improving equality in first-team football by introducing a recruitment code into regulation in order to help address the under-representation of BAME managers," an EFL statement read.

Gelson Martins throws doubt on AS Monaco future

Speaking to Record, 24-year-old attacker and Portuguese international Gelson Martins poured doubt on his future at AS Monaco, despite the Principality side telling supporters that the Ligue 1 club had purchased the player from Atletico Madrid.

“The truth is that Monaco already wanted me to sign before I joined Atletico Madrid. Their interest is well-documented, and as Leonardo Jardim made his return to the touchline, I decided to join Monaco. Over there, it was different. The team was on a bad run, the pressure was great, but I came for playing time and to enjoy myself. It was the best decision that I could have taken.”

“We will see what happens next. Monaco want me to stay but we will have to see. At Monaco, I played to not go down a division and I finished by receiving more attention. I did of course score some goals, but I can see that it was not enough to be called up for the Nations League.”

“I do not want to return to Atletico Madrid. I am still the same player and I know that my characteristics do not correspond with what the team wanted. And it is not now that things are going to change. Simeone is not going to change his philosophy for me.”

MOURINHO: I SEE MYSELF MORE WITH A NATIONAL TEAM

Jose Mourinho says he is open to returning to the game as a manager of a national team rather than a club.

The Portuguese has been out of management since being sacked by Manchester United in December after overseeing the club’s worst start to a campaign in 28 years.

When asked about his next challenge, Mourinho said he would welcome the opportunity to manage at international level and revealed it would not necessarily have to be with Portugal.

“I want to compete in new competitions,” the 56-year-old told Eleven Sports. “I think about the World Cup and the European Championships.

“For a long time I have had the desire to try out such an adventure. Right now, I see myself more at a national team than with a new club.

“Is Portugal the right team for me? Not necessarily.”

Milan, meeting between Maldini and Theo Hernandez: the images from Chiringuito TV


Milan is contuining its work on the transfer market this weekend. As seen on Chiringuito TV, a famous Spanish show, this Sunday morning Paolo Maldini had a meeting in Ibiza with Theo Hernandez, defender owned by Real Madrid.
